examples: To see the problem here more clearly, let's look at a different biological system, say, an acorn.
The chipmunk population increased strikingly due to two successive years of abundant acorn crops in the forest.
If an ant finds an outstanding hole—such as the inside of an acorn or a rock crevice—it recruits another scout to check it out.
In 1992, he headed a registration effort for Project Vote, an Acorn partner at the time.
In the 2008 election, Acorn's practices led to investigations, some ongoing, in 14 other states.
